parameters.jsCode:
const d = $('Parse Job Fields').first().json;
const cv = (d.cv_text || '').substring(0, 2000);

const _prompt =
  'You are a senior technical interviewer with 15+ years at Google, Meta, and NVIDIA Israel. '
  + 'You have conducted hundreds of interviews for junior software engineering roles.\n\n'
  + 'I have an interview for ' + d.job_title + ' at ' + d.company_name + '.\n'
  + 'Job requirements: ' + d.job_requirements + '\n\n'
  + 'My CV:\n' + cv + '\n\n'
  + 'Generate the following sections:\n\n'

  + '## 1. 60-Second Introduction\n'
  + 'Write the EXACT words I should say. Lead with my strongest relevant achievement. '
  + 'Second sentence: what I build and my domain. '
  + 'Third sentence: one specific reason I want to work at ' + d.company_name + ' (technical, not generic). '
  + 'Banned: excited, passionate, would love, looking forward.\n\n'

  + '## 2. Job Requirements → Interview Questions\n'
  + 'For EACH requirement listed in the job description, generate:\n'
  + '  - The most likely interview question based on that requirement '
  + '(use "Tell me about a time when..." for experience-based, '
  + '"How would you..." for hypothetical/design, '
  + '"Walk me through..." for technical deep-dives)\n'
  + '  - My best answer based on my ACTUAL CV above. '
  + 'Use the STAR format (Situation, Task, Action, Result) for behavioral. '
  + 'Use specific project names, real metrics, and technical decisions from my CV. '
  + 'Never invent experience I do not have — if I lack it, show how my closest experience is transferable.\n\n'

  + '## 3. Technical Deep-Dive Questions\n'
  + 'For my 2-3 most relevant projects for this role: '
  + 'Write the hardest technical follow-up question an interviewer would ask, '
  + 'and my ideal answer showing engineering depth (trade-offs, alternatives considered, lessons learned).\n\n'

  + '## 4. Questions I Should Ask the Interviewer\n'
  + '3-4 questions specific to ' + d.company_name + ' at the ' + d.job_title + ' level. '
  + 'These should reveal: their engineering culture, technical direction, how they handle scale. '
  + 'NOT: salary, work-life balance, perks.\n\n'

  + '## 5. Honest Weakness\n'
  + 'One genuine gap relevant to this role, framed as growth in progress. '
  + 'Specific to my actual CV — not generic. Show what I am actively doing about it.\n\n'
  + '## 6. Why Should We Hire You?\n'
  + 'Identify 3 key things I want the interviewer to walk out remembering about me. '
  + 'Choose based on: what is most relevant to ' + d.job_title + ' at ' + d.company_name + ', '
  + 'what genuinely differentiates me from other candidates at my level, '
  + 'and what I can actually prove with a story from my CV.\n'
  + 'For each of the 3 things:\n'
  + '  - State the attribute (e.g. "I deliver measurable performance results")\n'
  + '  - Write 1-2 specific stories from my CV that SHOW it (not tell it). '
  + 'Use real project names, real numbers, real decisions. '
  + 'Never invent. If a story does not exist in my CV, do not use it.\n'
  + '  - Write the exact 2-3 sentences I should say to open this point in the interview.\n\n'
  + '## 7. Why Do You Want to Work Here?\n'
  + 'Write a specific, researched answer for why I want to work at ' + d.company_name + ' '
  + 'in the ' + d.job_title + ' role specifically. '
  + 'Structure:\n'
  + '  Part 1 — The company angle: one specific technical thing ' + d.company_name + ' is doing '
  + '(a product, an engineering decision, a research area, a system they built) '
  + 'that genuinely connects to my work. Not "you are innovative" — something real and specific.\n'
  + '  Part 2 — The role angle: how this specific role (' + d.job_title + ') '
  + 'matches a specific skill or interest I have demonstrated in my CV. '
  + 'Connect it explicitly: "the work on X in my CV maps directly to Y that this role focuses on".\n'
  + '  Part 3 — The growth angle: what I expect to learn or build here that I cannot build elsewhere. '
  + 'Be specific to ' + d.company_name + '\'s scale, domain, or engineering culture.\n'
  + 'Write the exact words I should say — natural, confident, not rehearsed-sounding. '
  + 'Max 4-5 sentences total. No filler, no flattery.';

return [{ json: {
  prompt: _prompt,
  body: JSON.stringify({
    model: 'llama-3.1-8b-instant',
    messages: [{ role: 'user', content: _prompt }],
    temperature: 0.7,
    max_tokens: 4096
  })
} }];

parameters.subject: =✅ Job Application Complete: {{ $json.company_name }} - {{ $json.job_title }} | Fit: {{ $json.fit_score }}%
parameters.html:
=

✓ Application Complete

Company:{{ $json.company_name }}
Position:{{ $json.job_title }}
Location:{{ $json.location }}
Applied:{{ $json.applied_date }}
Job Fit Score:{{ $json.fit_score }}%
ATS Score:{{ $json.ats_score }}%
Git Status:{{ $json.git_status }}

Files saved to: {{ $json.vault_path }}

Follow-Up Schedule

  • Day 3: {{ $json.followup_day3 }}
  • Day 7: {{ $json.followup_day7 }}
  • Day 30: {{ $json.followup_day30 }}
  • Day 90: {{ $json.followup_day90 }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ message: 'Tailored resume: ' + $('Tier 6 - Tailored Resume').first().json.tailored_resume_path, content: $('Tier 6 - Tailored Resume').first().json.tailored_resume_content_base64, sha: $('Get Resume SHA').first().json.sha || undefined, author: { name: 'Odeliya Charitonova', email: 'odaliya12@gmail.com' } }) }}
